intro: Molinaea tolambitou, also known as Tolamba, is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, native to Madagascar. Traditionally, Tolamba has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and rheumatism, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Molinaea tolambitou include alkaloids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
